title: Rinorea caudata
taxonomy: Rinorea caudata is a plant species within the family Violaceae. It belongs to the order Malpighiales. The family Violaceae is a part of the larger clade rosids. This plant is further classified as belonging to the family Violaceae in the clade eurosids I. Rinorea caudata is classified as belonging to the clade eurosids I in the larger clade rosids.

intro: Rinorea caudata is a tropical plant species commonly known as "Caudata" or "Rinorea", belonging to the family Violaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues, thanks to its potential anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. Bioactive constituents of Rinorea caudata include alkaloids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal effects. In traditional herbal medicine, Rinorea caudata is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, with the dried roots and leaves being commonly used in these preparations.
